full_text:
GeoMx® DSP Hibernating Procedure for Extended Shutdown

MAN-10155-04

If the GeoMx DSP instrument will be inactive for 2 weeks or more, NanoString recommends following this hibernation protocol to flush the fluidic lines of instrument buffers and fill the lines with DEPC-treated (or other nuclease-free) water. When desired, the system can be returned to operational as described below.

(Please note that this procedure was revised in August 2023, and therefore differs from the procedure printed in the GeoMx DSP Instrument User Manual for v3.0 (July 2023). The procedure in the user manual will be updated when the manual is next revised.)

Materials

Two 1 L bottles of DEPC-treated or other nuclease-free water. Note: Thermo Fisher® AM9922 Ambion® DEPCTreated Water (1 L) has the correct threading to match the Buffer S bottle tops. An alternative to commercial DEPC-treated water is DEPC-treated water prepared in-house, following standard lab protocols.

1 spare tissue slide (for example, the tissue slide used in GeoMx training). A blank slide with a small indelible pen mark in the center and in the label area may suffice. • 1 spare collection plate.

Hibernate the system

Switch Buffer S bottles to DEPC-treated water bottles. Perform these steps without launching the Change Reagents wizard:

Open the bottle bay door and unscrew the bottle cap assemblies from the Buffer S bottles in bottle slots 1 and 4.

Replace these two bottles with DEPC-treated water. Close the bottle bay door.

Cap and save your bottles of Buffer S to use when returning the system to operational.

Prime lines:

From the Administration menu, select Admin Scripts (you must be logged in as an administrative user). From the dropdown menu, select Admin_SystemPrimeFromBottle. In the field for Parameter Value, enter 1. Click Proceed. The instrument primes the Bottle 1 line (allow 8 minutes to complete). NOTE THAT THERE IS NO SIGN SHOWING WHEN COMPLETE

Leave the Admin Scripts page, then select Admin Scripts again. Enter Parameter Value of 4. Click Proceed. The instrument primes the Bottle 4 line (allow 8 minutes to complete). ). NOTE THAT THERE IS NO SIGN SHOWING WHEN COMPLETE

Begin a mock run to push water through fluidic lines: [INSERT ONE SLIDE ONLY!! And make sure it is very dirty]

Start a new run by clicking New Data Collection, or Data Collection then New/Continue Run. If these buttons are not available, click Assist (wrench icon)  Restart Data Collection Workflow. When prompted:

Insert slide holder with a spare slide in slot 1, covered with DEPC-treated water.

Insert the spare collection plate.

Select any Morphology Marker kit from the dropdown menu. Do not select any Probe Kit. The system will warn that you will not be able to collect from this slide.

Proceed to the low magnification scan.

Define a scan area in the low magnification scan to proceed to the high magnification scan.

When the high magnification scan is complete, the system will initiate cleaning and move to the Collection Complete idle software state.

FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY. Not for use in diagnostic procedures. August 2023

GeoMx DSP Hibernating Procedure for Extended Shutdown MAN-10155-04

Once at the Collection Complete idle software state, click on Remove Slides and Microplate.

When prompted, open the door to retrieve the slide holder. Store the slide, thoroughly clean the slide holder, and return the holder to inside the instrument.

Remove the collection plate and keep as a spare.

When finished, close the door to the instrument.

Remember cleaning the gasket with DEPC treated water

To refresh at any tab, press F5

Turn off the instrument (optional):

Select the Administration tab from the GeoMx DSP Control Center.

Select System Settings  System Shutdown  Shutdown System.

When the screen turns black (after ~30 seconds), power off by flipping the switch on the back-right of the instrument.

Return the system to operational

Switch DEPC-treated water bottles to Buffer S bottles. Perform these steps without launching the Change Reagents wizard:

Open the bottle bay door and unscrew the bottle cap assemblies from the DEPC-treated water bottles in bottle slots 1 and 4.

Replace these two bottles with the Buffer S bottles that were removed previously. Close the bottle bay door.

Power On the instrument, if it was off:

Flip the switch on the back-right of the instrument to On. The system will boot up, which may take a few minutes.

Log in to the Control Center using an administrative user account.

Prime lines:

From the Administration menu, select Admin Scripts. From the dropdown menu, select Admin_SystemPrimeFromBottle. In the field for Parameter Value, enter 1. Click Proceed. The instrument primes the Bottle 1 line (allow 8 minutes to complete).

Leave the Admin Scripts page, then select Admin Scripts again. Enter Parameter Value of 4. Click Proceed. The instrument primes the Bottle 4 line (allow 8 minutes to complete).

Begin a mock run to push buffer through fluidic lines:

Start a new run by clicking New Data Collection; or Data Collection then New/Continue Run. If these buttons are not available, click Assist (wrench icon)  Restart Data Collection Workflow. When prompted:

Insert slide holder with a spare slide in slot 1, covered with Buffer S.

Insert the spare collection plate.

Select any Morphology Marker kit from the dropdown menu. Do not select any Probe Kit. The system will warn that you will not be able to collect from this slide.

Proceed through the Preparing Instrument and Initializing Fluidics steps to generate a low magnification scan.

Define a scan area in the low magnification scan to proceed to the high magnification scan.

When the high magnification scan is complete, the system will initiate cleaning and move to the Collection Complete idle software state.

Once at the Collection Complete idle software state, click on Remove Slides and Microplate.

When prompted, open the door to retrieve the slide holder. Store the slide, thoroughly clean the slide holder, and return the holder to inside the instrument.

You may remove the spare collection plate (and save for similar uses).

When finished, close the door to the instrument.

If the instrument was kept on during the hibernation, it is recommended to restart it before the next run. Contact Support@nanostring.com for technical support.

FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY. Not for use in diagnostic procedures.

2
